code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about airports (or related entities) into a single
    fluent, complex sentence. Unknown predicates fall back to a generic phrasing.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes if present."""
        return val.strip('"').strip("'")

    # Helper to build a clause for a single triple
    def _clause(s: str, p: str, o: str) -> str:
        """Return a natural‑language fragment for a single triple."""
        # Normalise object string
        o = o.strip('"').strip("'")
        # Simple mappings
        simple_map = {
            "cityServed": f"{s} serves the city of {o}",
            "location": f"{s} is located in {o}",
            "country": f"{s} is in {o}",
            "isPartOf": f"{s} is part of {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": f"{s} lies at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": f"{s} lies at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
            "operatingOrganisation": f"{s} is operated by {o}",
            "runwayName": f"{s}'s runway is named {o}",
            "runwayLength": f"{s}'s runway length is {o}",
            "runwaySurfaceType": f"{s} has a runway surface of {o}",
            "iataLocationIdentifier": f"{s} has IATA code {o}",
            "icaoLocationIdentifier": f"{s} has ICAO code {o}",
            "locationIdentifier": f"{s} has the location identifier {o}",
            "nativeName": f"{s} is also known as {o}",
            "leader": f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
            "leaderParty": f"The leader of {s} belongs to {o}",
            "capital": f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
            "language": f"The language spoken in {s} is {o}",
            "officialLanguage": f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
            "currency": f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
            "owner": f"{s} is owned by {o}",
            "headquarter": f"The headquarters of {s} is at {o}",
            "foundingYear": f"{s} was founded in {o}",
            "postalCode": f"The postal code of {s} is {o}",
            "areaCode": f"The area code of {s} is {o}",
            "jurisdiction": f"{s} falls under the jurisdiction of {o}",
            "demonym": f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
            "largestCity": f"The largest city of {s} is {o}",
            "countySeat": f"The county seat of {s} is {o}",
            "chief": f"The chief of {s} is {o}",
            "foundedBy": f"{s} was founded by {o}",
            "regionServed": f"{s} serves the region of {o}",
            "hubAirport": f"{s} uses {o} as a hub airport",
            "aircraftHelicopter": f"{s} operates the helicopter {o}",
            "transportAircraft": f"{s} operates the transport aircraft {o}",
            "aircraftFighter": f"{s} operates the fighter aircraft {o}",
            "attackAircraft": f"{s} operates the attack aircraft {o}",
            "battle": f"{s} participated in the battle of {o}",
        }
        if p in simple_map:
            return simple_map[p]

        # Runway specific patterns
        if p.endswith("RunwayNumber"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway number is {o}"
        if p.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway is {o} feet long"
        if p.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway is {o} metres long"
        if p.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway surface is {o}"
        if p.endswith("RunwayName"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway is named {o}"
        # Fallback generic phrasing
        return f"{s} has {p} {o}"

    # Build clauses
    clauses: list[str] = [_clause(t.subject, t.predicate, _clean(t.object)) for t in triples]

    # Combine clauses into a single complex sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
